checks.finding: No AC-NNN identifiers appear in any task's `Requirements:` field (T-001..T-004 Requirements fields cite only REQ-001..REQ-006). Broadening to all AC-NNN references anywhere in tasks.md (Done When / Scope parentheticals), every cited ID (AC-001..AC-018) resolves to a real row in acceptance-tests.md's table (AC-001..AC-018, verified by grep diff). No dangling AC ID found.

T-001 Blockers: `None`; T-002 Blockers: `T-001`; T-003 Blockers: `T-001, T-002`; T-004 Blockers: `T-001, T-002` (tasks.md lines 187, 316, 456, 600). All match the required `None` or comma-separated `T-\d{3}` list format; no range notation or prose. Confirms precheck-result.json `blockers_format_valid: true`.

REQ-001..REQ-006 (requirements.md Goals section) each appear in at least one task's Requirements field: REQ-001->T-001, REQ-002->T-002, REQ-003->T-003, REQ-004->T-004, REQ-005->T-001..T-004, REQ-006->T-001..T-004 (tasks.md lines 69, 212, 342, 481). No uncovered requirement.

AC-001..AC-018 (acceptance-tests.md) are each traced to a task via tasks.md Done When items and confirmed in traceability.md's Acceptance Mapping table (lines 37-54), e.g. AC-001->T-001, AC-005->T-002, AC-009->T-003, AC-011->T-004, AC-017/AC-014/AC-015/AC-016 mapped to all four tasks with per-task scope notes. No uncovered AC.

T-001 Requirements: REQ-001, REQ-005, REQ-006; T-002: REQ-002, REQ-005, REQ-006; T-003: REQ-003, REQ-005, REQ-006; T-004: REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006 (tasks.md lines 69, 212, 342, 481). Every task references at least one REQ-NNN; no orphan task.

Task-title substring scan (tasks.md lines 48, 191, 320, 460) finds only T-002 ('...its spec-review smoke suite') matching the 'spec' substring; no task's scope is exclusively test-file-only (each includes CHANGELOG/docs/ADR/evidence-log deliverables), so no other task is classified as a test task by the scope criterion. T-002's Done When cites AC-005, AC-006, AC-007, AC-017 (tasks.md lines 282-297), so it is not orphaned from acceptance criteria.
T-001..T-004 each declare `Approval: Draft` and `Status: Planned` (tasks.md lines 52/54, 195/197, 324/326, 464/466), matching the round-1/attempt-1 requirement.

All four tasks declare `Risk: medium` and `Required Workflow: acceptance-first` (tasks.md lines 56/63, 199/206, 328/336, 468/475), which is the correct risk-gate-matrix pairing (medium -> acceptance-first). Consistent with precheck-result.json `workflow_match_precheck: PASS`.

AC-001..AC-013 and AC-018 are each claimed as primary test target by exactly one task (traceability.md Acceptance Mapping, lines 37-54). AC-014/AC-015/AC-016/AC-017 are cited in more than one task's Done When (tasks.md lines 168-176, 298-306, 436-444, 581-589), but each citation is explicitly scoped to that task's own deliverable ('for this task's twins', 'share for this task', '<suite> leg' per-suite runtime assertion) rather than re-testing the identical target twice at the same level — matching the shared-AC-with-distinct-scope exception.

Blockers references T-001 (from T-002, T-003, T-004) and T-002 (from T-003, T-004) all resolve to existing task IDs, confirmed against dependency-graph.json `nodes: ["T-001","T-002","T-003","T-004"]`. No dangling task reference.

BLOCKERS-FORMAT is PASS, so this check runs. dependency-graph.json edges (T-002->T-001, T-003->T-001, T-003->T-002, T-004->T-001, T-004->T-002) form a DAG rooted at T-001 with no back-edge; no cycle detected.

T-001's 'inventory and its registration-forcing suite' and T-002's 'driver and its spec-review smoke suite' pair an artifact with directly-tied verification work for that same artifact (acceptable pattern 1). T-003's 'suite and record the ... RED differential' pairs the suite with its own regression-evidence recording (acceptable pattern 1). T-004 bundles the escalation leg, the template<->gate parity extension, and the python3-absent degradation path, but all three trace to the single REQ-004 ('Add the quality-gate escalation leg and the template<->gate parity EXTENSION...', requirements.md REQ-004) and design.md's explicit 'A4 parity-extension placement (decision)' section, which deliberately co-locates them in one suite/one issue (#144) rather than being an arbitrary merge of unrelated features by this task decomposition.

Each of T-001, T-002, T-003, T-004's CHANGELOG/AC-016 Done When bullet (tasks.md lines 171-173, 301-303, 439-441, 584-586) reads: '`CHANGELOG.md` `## Unreleased` contains an entry citing #<issue>, the REQ-006 documents affected by this task are updated in the same PR, and `tests/validate-repository.sh` exits 0'. The middle clause, 'the REQ-006 documents affected by this task are updated in the same PR,' is an unmeasurable 'update <X>' criterion per the calibration's forbidden-phrase list: it names no exact file or section from the REQ-006 candidate list (README.md / USERGUIDE.md / docs/workflow-guide.md / docs/skill-reference.md / docs/agent-capability-matrix.md / PLUGIN-CONTRACTS.md / docs/troubleshooting.md / docs/contributor/*) and no content change that proves the update, so an implementer or independent quality-gate reviewer cannot determine which document(s) apply to this specific task or what change satisfies it. The bullet's other two clauses (CHANGELOG grep-target, validate-repository.sh exit code) are concrete, but this clause is not. All four tasks repeat the identical unmeasurable phrasing.

traceability.md's Task Mapping table (lines 27-31) lists T-001..T-004, matching tasks.md; its Requirement rows (lines 8-13) cite REQ-001..REQ-006, all present in requirements.md. Full-profile layer-spec anchors verified as canonical headings in the hash-bound layer docs: infra-spec.md#cicd-sequence, infra-spec.md#runtime-dependencies, infra-spec.md#deployment-topology (from '## CI/CD Sequence', '## Runtime Dependencies', '## Deployment Topology'); security-spec.md#trust-boundaries, security-spec.md#security-tests, security-spec.md#authorization (from '## Trust Boundaries', '## Security Tests', '## Authorization'). REQ-006's Layer Spec cell uses the required 'N/A — cross-layer only: <reason>' form. No dangling reference either direction.

findings.finding: tasks.md Done When bullets at lines 171-173 (T-001, citing #141), 301-303 (T-002, citing #142), 439-441 (T-003, citing #143), and 584-586 (T-004, citing #144) each contain the clause 'the REQ-006 documents affected by this task are updated in the same PR' with no named file/section and no stated content change that proves completion. This is the calibration's forbidden 'update <X> without a measurable target' pattern. Recommend naming, per task, which of the REQ-006 candidate documents (README.md / USERGUIDE.md / docs/workflow-guide.md / docs/skill-reference.md / docs/agent-capability-matrix.md / PLUGIN-CONTRACTS.md / docs/troubleshooting.md / docs/contributor/*) actually apply and what section/content change in each proves the update, or state 'no REQ-006 documents apply to this task' if none do.
